title: Clinically relevant endpoints and quality-of-life outcomes with darolutamide in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer: Analyses of the phase III ARASENS trial.
abstract: Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer (mHSPC) require long-term treatment to delay progression and improve survival, while minimizing adverse events or negative impact on symptoms (e.g. pain) and health-related quality of life (HRQoL). In ARASENS (NCT02799602), darolutamide plus androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) and docetaxel significantly improved overall survival (primary endpoint) versus placebo plus ADT and docetaxel. We report clinically relevant endpoints and HRQoL in ARASENS.
